steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank falls between 'French portrait artist' and 'Marie-Denise Villers (1774–1821).' The name is an appositive — it identifies which artist is meant. The question is which punctuation (or no punctuation) correctly joins a restrictive appositive.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — em dash: an em dash introduces a nonrestrictive aside or elaboration, not a simple identifier. ✗
  • Choice B — no punctuation: a restrictive appositive that identifies exactly which person is meant takes no punctuation. 'Marie-Denise Villers' tells us which artist — it is essential, not decorative. ✓
  • Choice C — colon: a colon follows an independent clause to introduce a list or explanation; the name here is a direct identifier, not a listed elaboration. ✗
  • Choice D — comma: a comma signals a nonrestrictive appositive (removable extra info), but the name cannot be removed without losing the subject entirely. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
